subject: [PATCH v3 00/11] mm: PMD-level swap entries for anonymous THPs

reviews.author: Usama Arif (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed a concern about the PMD swap entry detection support by explaining that it currently splits PMD-mapped THPs into PTE-level swap entries, but later patches will install single PMD-level swap entries instead. The author added infrastructure to detect those entries and provided generic no-op PMD swap exclusive fallbacks for architectures without PMD softleaf support.

reviews.author: Usama Arif (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed a concern about the handling of PMD swap entries in __split_huge_pmd_locked() by adding a new branch that splits a PMD swap entry into 512 PTE swap entries, preserving soft_dirty, uffd_wp, and exclusive flags. The author acknowledged that page and folio are left uninitialized in this branch to avoid freezing=true, but added a VM_WARN_ON_ONCE(freeze) to catch any future caller that breaks this invariant.

reviews.author: Usama Arif (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed a concern about handling PMD swap entries during fork and copy_huge_pmd(). They taught copy_huge_non_present_pmd() to duplicate PMD swap entries instead of splitting them, mirroring the behavior for PTEs. The function now returns an error if it fails to allocate memory for the swap table, and retries with GFP_KERNEL allocation. This change preserves the THP during fork.

reviews.author: Usama Arif (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed a concern about zswap's handling of large folios, explaining that they should only be swapped in when their whole range is expected to be on disk and adding a new function zswap_range_has_entry() to check if any slot in the range is still in zswap. The author confirmed that PMD swap-entry consumers will split before calling into PMD-order swapin whenever any slot is still in zswap, ensuring safe handling of large folios.

reviews.author: Usama Arif (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed a concern about handling PMD swap entries during swapoff by adding unuse_pmd() and calling it from unuse_pmd_range(). This mirrors the existing unuse_pte_range(), but operates at PMD granularity. The function returns 0 on success, -EAGAIN if the swap cache folio no longer matches the entry or the PMD changed under the lock, and -EIO if the folio is not uptodate.

reviews.author: Usama Arif (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed a concern about handling PMD swap entries in various mm functions, specifically smaps_pmd_entry(), clear_soft_dirty_pmd(), and madvise_free_huge_pmd(). They added new helpers to account for swap entries in smaps_account_swap() and modified several functions to handle PMD swap entries correctly. The author confirmed that the changes will be included in v2 of the patch series.

reviews.author: Usama Arif (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed a concern about MADV_WILLNEED on PMD swap entries, explaining that swapin_walk_pmd_entry() skips non-present PMDs and thus MADV_WILLNEED is a no-op. They also described how to handle PMD swap entries under pmd_trans_huge_lock(), including pinning the swap device and reading the folio in at PMD order if necessary.

reviews.author: Usama Arif (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed a concern about move_pages_huge_pmd() returning -ENOENT for non-trans_huge, non-migration PMDs by adding a new function move_swap_pmd() that moves the swap entry whole-PMD and re-anchors the anon rmap. The dispatcher in move_pages_huge_pmd() now waits for migration on a PMD migration entry and routes PMD swap entries through move_swap_pmd().
sentiment_signals: added new code to address it

reviews.author: Usama Arif (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed a concern about handling PMD swap entries in the fault path, explaining that they added a new handler do_huge_pmd_swap_page() to directly map PMD-sized folios from the swap cache and split the entry into PTE-level swap entries when necessary. The author confirmed that this approach mirrors the existing PTE-path handling for swap-in.

2026-07-03.patch_summary: This patch series introduces a new feature to Linux kernel called PMD-level swap entries for anonymous THPs (Transparent Huge Pages). When a PMD-mapped anonymous THP is swapped out, the current implementation splits it into PTE-level swap entries. This patch series changes this behavior so that a single PMD-level swap entry is created instead, allowing the huge mapping to survive the swap round-trip and be restored directly on swap-in without needing khugepaged to collapse the range later. The PMD swap entry is a compact page-table encoding for HPAGE_PMD_NR consecutive swap slots, and its handling is designed to work seamlessly with existing kernel features like zswap and khugepaged.

reviews.author: Andrew Morton
reviews.summary: Reviewer Andrew Morton asked the maintainers to review existing code, specifically pointing out a potential issue in move_pages_huge_pmd() where a stack-local pgtable_t* is passed to pmd_migration_entry_wait(), and suggested that AI review may have found additional issues
reviews.raw_body:
Thanks.

AI review said a lot about the new code.

It also might have found a bunch of issues in the existing code.  I
looked at one (move_pages_huge_pmd() passing a stack-local pgtable_t*
to pmd_migration_entry_wait()) and it looks legit.

So can the people who work on this code please take a look?

	https://sashiko.dev/#/patchset/20260703173903.3789516-1-usama.arif@linux.dev

2026-07-04.patch_summary: This patch series introduces a new feature to Linux kernel called PMD-level swap entries for anonymous THPs (Transparent Huge Pages). When a PMD-mapped anonymous THP is swapped out, the current implementation splits it into PTE-level swap entries. This patch series changes this behavior so that a single PMD-level swap entry is created instead, allowing the huge mapping to survive the swap round-trip and be restored directly on swap-in without needing khugepaged to collapse the range later. The PMD swap entry is a compact page-table encoding for HPAGE_PMD_NR consecutive swap slots, and its handling is designed to work seamlessly with existing kernel features like zswap and khugepaged.

reviews.author: Kairui Song
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Kairui Song noted that the patch series does not include a test case to verify PMD swap entry handling, and requested that one be added.

Reviewer noted that swapin_sync() does a sync swap-in without readahead, which makes sense due to the PMD's large size, but suggested mentioning this in the commit message for clarity and proposed using the return value of swapin_sync() directly instead of checking its result.

Reviewer Kairui Song expressed concern that try locks in PMD-sized folio handling could fail more frequently on slower devices, potentially leading to repeated faults.
reviews.raw_body:
Hello Usama

Amazing work.

---

Hmm, so here we always do a sync swapin which means there is no
readahead, kind of make sense since PMD is huge, readahead might be
not helpful and a order 0 readahead breaks following PMD. But maybe
worth mention it in the commit message at least so it's easiler to
follow. (partly because the name swapin_sync is a bit confusing at the
moment, we could change that name as we improve swap readahead later).

And just an idea that will it be suffient if we just use the return
value of swapin_sync directly? If a smaller folio covers the PMD start
entry, the smaller folio is returned and you catched that just fine.
If a smaller folio covers a follow-up sub-entry or a sub-entry is
freed, -EBUSY is returned here and you just do the split. And it
doesn't do the allocation for the first lookup check, so there should
be no thrashing issue.

---

With PMD sized folio on a slower device, this try lock could fail much
more frequently, leading to repeated faults. That's not a new issue
and I remember Barry talked about it at the LSFMM this year, just I'm
not sure if that will get much worse for PMD?

2026-07-07.patch_summary: This patch series introduces a new feature to Linux kernel called PMD-level swap entries for anonymous THPs (Transparent Huge Pages). When a PMD-mapped anonymous THP is swapped out, the current implementation splits it into PTE-level swap entries. This patch series changes this behavior so that a single PMD-level swap entry is created instead, allowing the huge mapping to survive the swap round-trip and be restored directly on swap-in without needing khugepaged to collapse the range later. The PMD swap entry is a compact page-table encoding for HPAGE_PMD_NR consecutive swap slots, and its handling is designed to work seamlessly with existing kernel features like zswap and khugepaged.

reviews.author: Usama Arif (author)
reviews.summary:
Author acknowledged that the swapoff path needs to drop the per-vswap spinlock before calling try_to_unmap(), agreed to restructure in v2.

Author agreed to address two concerns: adding a mention of swapoff path handling in the commit message and acknowledging that readahead is not suitable for PMD-level swap entries.

The author is addressing a concern about zswap's handling of PMD swap entries, specifically that calling PMD-order swapin_sync() when the cache is empty but any slot is in zswap can leave a non-uptodate PMD-sized folio in swapcache. The author acknowledges this issue and plans to address it.

The author acknowledged a potential issue with PMD-sized IO keeping a folio locked longer, especially on slow swap devices, but agreed that the current behavior is acceptable and any improvement should be addressed in broader swap readahead/async swapin work.
reviews.raw_body:
Hi Kairui,

Thanks! and Thanks for the review comments!

---

Yes, I\u2019ll add this to the commit message. Thanks!

Yes readahead won't be useful for PMD as you said.

---

The main issue over here is zswap.
zswap still stores the range as order-0 objects. If we call PMD-order
swapin_sync() when the cache is empty but any slot is in zswap, zswap_load()
fails the large-folio load and leaves a non-uptodate PMD-sized folio in
swapcache. Splitting the PMD after that is not enough unless we also delete that
failed large folio, because the subsequent PTE fault can find the non-uptodate
large folio and SIGBUS instead of loading the individual zswap entries.

---

Agreed that PMD-sized IO can keep the folio locked longer, especially on
slow swap devices. (Which hopefully are dying away :))
The current behavior matches do_swap_page(): on the normal retryable fault
path it drops the fault lock and waits for the folio unlock before returning
VM_FAULT_RETRY, so it should not be a tight retry loop.
The wait can be longer for 2MB IO, but the retry usually comes back to
either an uptodate folio or a PMD that another fault already  installed.
I think this is the right baseline behavior, and any improvement here
belongs with the broader swap readahead / async swapin work.

reviews.author: Yosry Ahmed
reviews.summary: Reviewer Yosry Ahmed suggested using zswap_is_present() instead of implementing a custom range lookup function in patch 4 (mm: zswap: add range lookup for large-folio swapin), and provided a URL to a previous email that proposed this interface
reviews.raw_body:
Maybe zswap_is_present() as suggested here:
https://lore.kernel.org/linux-mm/CAO9r8zNni28A+9BiDgDTBRuvGTKnqH91kpbpjjeftfnKSeSOJg@mail.gmail.com/.

IIUC this interface can be used for the same purpose, we just need to
pass nr=1.

The PMD swap entry is a compact page-table encoding for HPAGE_PMD_NR consecutive swap slots, and its handling is designed to work seamlessly with existing kernel features like zswap and khugepaged.

reviews.author: Usama Arif (author)
reviews.summary: The author acknowledged that they forgot to format the commit message and promised to fix it in the next revision.
reviews.raw_body:
I forgot to format the commit message before sending this patch,
sorry about this, will fix in the next revision.
